v2.label: tab-compare
buttonsSample:
Incapacity standard
Attorney decision
Trigger definition

New drafting instruction asks for consistent incapacity explanations in the client-facing summary.

Review
High
Trust

Trust requires two licensed physicians or court determination before successor trustee acts.

Financial POA

POA becomes effective immediately, with optional physician certification for third-party reliance.

AHCD

AHCD lets the agent act when the principal cannot make health care decisions.

The legal triggers do not need to be identical, but the attorney should explain why health, financial, and trust triggers differ.

Spousal waiver boundaries
Attorney decision
Prenup constraint

New drafting instruction requires the estate plan to honor, not silently expand, prenup waivers.

Review
Low
Prenup

Prenup waives elective-share-style claims only within the agreement scope and preserves disclosure exhibits.

Trust / Will

Estate documents include spouse as beneficiary in selected circumstances but do not cite prenup exhibits.

Financial POA

POA grants broad gifting authority that could affect separate-property boundaries.

Attorney should confirm whether gifting powers, trust distributions, and waivers remain inside the negotiated prenup boundaries.

Health care authority and privacy release
Attorney decision
Authority gap

Attorney-provided update calls for modern privacy authorization and agent-access language.

Add
Medium
AHCD

Directive gives broad treatment and end-of-life authority but uses older privacy-release phrasing.

Financial POA

Financial POA authorizes insurance and benefits administration but not medical treatment decisions.

Trust

Trust permits successor trustee to pay health expenses after incapacity.

Add a review item so the AHCD privacy release, insurance authority, and trustee payment authority work together without merging roles.

Successor fiduciary alignment
Approved
Decision-maker conflict

Attorney-provided update requires clear incapacity trigger and acceptance language for successor fiduciaries.

Revise
High
Trust

Trust names Maya Chen as first successor trustee and Daniel Chen as backup.

Financial POA / AHCD

Financial POA names Daniel as first agent; AHCD names Maya as first health care agent.

Prenup

Prenup has no fiduciary appointments, but requires separate-property tracking before transfers.

Keep the named people, but expose the mismatch so the attorney can confirm whether financial and health care authority should intentionally diverge.

Separate property funding guardrails
Approved
Property characterization

New drafting instruction: preserve prenup classifications in funding, schedules, and distribution notes.

Revise
High
Trust

Trust schedule funds community and separate assets into one revocable trust without a property-character legend.

Prenup

Prenup preserves premarital assets, appreciation, and listed accounts as separate property unless expressly transmuted.

Pour-over will

Will pours residue into the trust but does not restate property-character limitations.

The trust can remain the main vehicle, but funding language should not blur separate-property treatment created by the prenup.

Pour-over residuary alignment
Approved
Cross-document consistency

New execution packet should use one trust identity across will, trust, certificates, and signing memo.

Keep
Medium
Pour-over will

Will sends residue to the trust dated March 4, 2021, including later amendments.

Trust

Trust caption uses March 4, 2021, but amendment block references an April 2024 restatement.

Prenup

Prenup excludes several listed separate assets from any automatic community-property presumption.

The pour-over structure is sound; the generated packet should normalize the trust name/date and flag funding carveouts.
v2.bodyHead: + New chatDraft a documentDrafting MagicVerify citations OUTPUT TYPE: draft PACKET (3 sources, base: Source 1): ### Source: Source 1 [rol 4 msg · 13h ago In one sentence: who must verify discovery responses in California state court? 2 msg · 14h ago Use the CEB practice-guide tool to find guidance on California Probate Code 6111 4 msg · 14h ago Find the leading California Supreme Court anti-SLAPP case on protected activity. 4 msg · 14h ago Find the leading California Supreme Court anti-SLAPP case on protected activity. 4 msg · 14h ago Find the leading California Supreme Court anti-SLAPP case on protected activity. 4 msg · 14h ago In one sentence, who decides motions to compel in California Superior Court? 2 msg · 14h ago Test: send and clear 2 msg · 14h ago In one sentence, what is California Civil Code section 1942? 2 msg · 14h ago In one sentence, what is California Civil Code section 1942? 2 msg · 14h ago In one sentence, what is California Civil Code section 1942? 2 msg · 14h ago I

v2.label: tab-strategy
buttonsSample:
People and roles
Review

Trustee, executor, financial agent, health care agent, spouse, successors, and alternates.

AHCD and financial POA agent order mismatch

Trust identity
Normalize

Trust name, date, amendments, settlor/trustor language, and pour-over references.

Pour-over will should use the same operative trust identity

Property character
High risk

Separate property, community property, funding schedules, and prenup carveouts.

Prenup terms constrain trust funding language

Authority and powers
Map

Trustee powers, financial authority, health decisions, HIPAA release, and incapacity triggers.

Authority should be reconciled before drafting

Execution packet
Checklist

Signing memo, certificates, witnessing/notary requirements, and funding instructions.

Final draft should create attorney review gates
